name: Urolithin A trained runners performance and recovery trial
summary: Randomized double-blind placebo-controlled trial testing 1000 mg/day urolithin A for 4 weeks in competitive young male distance runners during altitude training, with running performance, recovery, mitochondrial biomarker, and muscle-biopsy endpoints.
population: 42 highly trained male distance runners aged 18 to 40 years

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are published in PMID 40839339; ClinicalTrials.gov has no posted results section.
trial_details.why_it_matters: The trial tests urolithin A performance, recovery, and mitochondrial biomarkers, but the young athletic population and non-significant primary performance result make it limiting context rather than aging efficacy evidence.
